title: Chicken and Broccoli Meal-Prep Bowl

subs.name: 1 cup quinoa for additional protein and a complete amino-acid profile
salt + a splash of vinegar — gluten-free and soy-free

steps.text: Cook the rice with 1.75 cups water and a pinch of salt — bring to a boil, reduce to low, cover and simmer 12-14 minutes until all water is absorbed. Fluff and set aside.

Flatten the chicken breast to an even thickness by placing it between plastic wrap and pressing gently with a rolling pin. In a bowl, toss chicken with garlic, soy sauce, lemon juice, black pepper and red chilli powder; leave for 5 minutes.

Heat 1 tbsp oil in a frying pan on medium-high. Add the chicken and cook undisturbed 6-7 minutes until golden on the underside. Flip and cook 5-6 minutes more until juices run clear. Transfer to a board and slice after 3 minutes of resting.

Add remaining 0.5 tbsp oil to the same pan on high heat. Toss in broccoli florets, season with salt and pepper, and stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until bright green with lightly charred tips. Do not overcook — the florets should still have a firm bite.

Divide rice into two bowls or meal-prep containers. Arrange chicken slices and broccoli alongside and drizzle any pan juices over the top. Seal and refrigerate for up to 3 days; reheat in the microwave or a steamer for 2 minutes before eating.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Verify poultry doneness with a food thermometer where possible; do not rely on colour alone.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: soy-sauce → salt + a splash of vinegar — gluten-free and soy-free (add an extra 0.5 tsp salt); broccoli → cauliflower, capsicum or green beans (cook time stays the same); rice → 1 cup quinoa for additional protein and a complete amino-acid profile
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Use a tawa instead of a frying pan for better caramelisation on the chicken. Replace soy sauce with 1 tsp ginger-garlic paste plus a dash of vinegar for an Indian-spiced version with no soy. Broccoli (called 'broccoli' in Indian markets) is now widely stocked in city sabzi mandis; substitute with cauliflower or beans if unavailable.
